skill.name: vercel-deployment
skill.description: Guides Vercel deployment planning for Next.js applications, including environments, runtimes, previews, monitoring, and production readiness.
skill.summary: Plan safer Next.js deployments on Vercel with practical runtime, environment, preview, and build guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command detections at SKILL.md:79 are Markdown code spans naming related skills, not executable shell usage. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance detections are also false positives from descriptive deployment text, with no prompt injection, secret access, command execution, or exfiltration behavior found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution twice, but SKILL.md:79 only uses Markdown inline code to list related skill names. No command is executed or requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks surround plain related-skill names in documentation. There is no shell syntax, interpolation, or instruction to run a command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3, but the line is frontmatter description text about Vercel deployment. No cryptographic algorithm is referenced.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only natural-language metadata for deployment topics. I found no hash, cipher, encoding routine, or security-sensitive computation there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Term
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:25, but the line says to choose the right Edge or Serverless runtime. It does not describe scanning hosts, ports, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term is used in normal Vercel architecture guidance. There is no network probing action, command, target, or collection instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Capability Keyword
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:35, but the line is a capability label for serverless deployment guidance. It contains no reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is a single deployment capability keyword. No executable workflow or malicious intent is present in the surrounding section.

content.value_statement: Production Vercel deployments need environment, runtime, build, and preview settings configured correctly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical Next.js deployment decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains Vercel deployment settings for Next.js App Router projects.
Guides environment variable separation for development, preview, and production.
Compares Edge and Serverless runtimes for API route decisions.
Highlights build optimization, caching, timeout, and bundle-size concerns.
Identifies deployment anti-patterns such as exposed NEXT_PUBLIC secrets.
limitations: Does not deploy applications by itself or run the Vercel CLI.
Does not verify account settings, project permissions, or billing limits.
Does not replace Vercel documentation for current platform limits.
Requires project details to give precise runtime and environment advice.

use_cases.title: Prepare a First Production Release
use_cases.description: Review environment variables, build settings, and preview workflows before moving a Next.js application to production.

use_cases.title: Choose Runtime Architecture
use_cases.description: Compare Edge and Serverless options for API routes, latency goals, platform limits, and Node.js API needs.

use_cases.title: Audit Deployment Risks
use_cases.description: Find common Vercel deployment mistakes, including public secrets, shared preview databases, stale caching, and oversized functions.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Next.js app for Vercel deployment readiness. Focus on environment variables, preview deployments, and production risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Environment Separation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design development, preview, and production environment variable handling for a Vercel-hosted Next.js App Router project.

prompt_templates.title: Select Function Runtime
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether my API routes should use Edge or Serverless on Vercel. Consider latency, dependencies, Node.js APIs, and timeouts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Deployment Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Vercel production deployment plan. Identify risks in caching, build size, preview databases, observability, and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: Check whether my project is ready for a first Vercel production deployment.
output: A readiness checklist covering environment variables, preview deployments, build settings, and monitoring.
A short list of blockers that should be fixed before production release.

output_examples.input: Help me decide between Edge and Serverless for an API route.
output: A runtime recommendation based on dependencies, latency needs, timeout risk, and Node.js API requirements.
A migration note if the current route is likely to break in Edge runtime.

output_examples.input: Find deployment anti-patterns in my Vercel setup.
output: A prioritized risk review for public secrets, preview database isolation, cache behavior, and function size.
Concrete remediation steps for each confirmed issue.

best_practices: Use separate environment variables and databases for preview and production deployments.
Choose Edge runtime only when dependencies and APIs are compatible.
Set up analytics, error tracking, and rollback steps before production traffic.
anti_patterns: Using the production database from preview deployments.
Ignoring function size, timeouts, cache behavior, and cold starts.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy my app automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides deployment guidance and review support, but it does not run deployment commands.

faq.question: Is this only for Next.js?
faq.answer: The skill is focused on Vercel deployments for Next.js, especially App Router projects.

faq.question: Can it help with environment variables?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to separate development, preview, and production variables safely.

faq.question: Can it choose Edge or Serverless runtime?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare runtime tradeoffs when you provide route behavior and dependency details.

faq.question: Does it check current Vercel platform limits?
faq.answer: No. Use current Vercel documentation for exact limits, pricing, and account-specific settings.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
